cross_scene_arcs.title: The Cracks Beneath the Surface: Jack's Performance of Success
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jack presents a façade of professional and personal fulfillment, but subtle inconsistencies reveal growing dissonance between his outward performance and inner turmoil. His relationship with Sam shifts from peer to hierarchical superior, yet his emotional investment in Sam's stagnation suggests unresolved personal insecurities.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Jack performs confidence and success in public, masking stress and emotional strain.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Jack fully aligns with institutional authority, adopting a subordinate role under Helpmann while maintaining a veneer of inclusion.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The authenticity of Jack's loyalty and contentment remains ambiguous, as does the extent to which he has sacrificed selfhood for systemic acceptance.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Colleague to Conspirator: Jack's Institutional Turn
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jack transitions from a passive bureaucrat to an active enforcer of systemic control, prioritizing protocol over personal bonds. His refusal to assist Sam and rejection of emotional appeals mark a definitive break, positioning him as an antagonist in Sam's quest for truth.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Jack seeks to contain the fallout of the Buttle/Tuttle error and maintain operational secrecy.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Jack severs all personal ties with Sam, choosing institutional survival over loyalty.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Jack acts out of genuine belief in the system or fear of personal consequence remains unclear.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Paranoia Arc: Order in Chaos or Delusion?
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jack evolves from managing a clerical error to interpreting it as part of a deliberate, hidden operation, revealing a shift in his cognitive framework. His goal becomes uncovering systemic conspiracy rather than correcting mistakes.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Jack treats the Buttle/Tuttle mix-up as a bureaucratic failure to be managed.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Jack believes the error is an intentional inside operation, reflecting a worldview defined by control and interconnected causality.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: It remains uncertain whether Jack's theory reflects insight into real corruption or a descent into institutional paranoia.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Final Confrontation: Duty, Identity, and Collapse
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jack is thrust into the role of interrogator and executioner of Sam, forcing a collision between personal history and professional duty. His emotional breakdown and ultimate acknowledgment of Sam's mental erasure mark the tragic culmination of his arc—complicity in the system's victory over individuality.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Jack attempts to maintain detachment while interrogating Sam under duress.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Jack witnesses Sam's complete psychological collapse, symbolizing the system's total control, and silently accepts the loss.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Jack's own psychological resistance or surrender to the system remains unexplored, leaving his final stance ambiguous.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Persistence of the Pursuer: Jack as Systemic Embodiment
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jack transforms from an unseen bureaucratic force into a tangible agent of pursuit, embodying the system's relentless reach. Even after failure, he remains a symbol of institutional continuity, confronting the limits of control when Sam escapes into fantasy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Jack actively pursues Sam, representing the system's attempt to reassert dominance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Jack acknowledges defeat as Sam escapes reality, marking the collapse of both physical and psychological control.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Jack will continue to serve as an instrument of the system or experience a crisis of faith remains open-ended.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Privacy to Persecution: Jill's Shattered Safety
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jill transitions from seeking personal privacy in her flat to becoming a fugitive after a violent intrusion and subsequent explosion destroy her home, marking a complete collapse of her civilian status and autonomy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Jill seeks normalcy and respite in her private space.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She is on the run, her home destroyed, and her life upended by unexplained state violence.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The motives behind the intrusion and explosion remain unknown, leaving Jill's safety and identity under constant threat.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Bureaucratic Rebellion: From Form to Fire
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jill's escalating resistance against an arbitrary bureaucratic system evolves from a simple request for a stamp to a high-stakes act of subversion, culminating in the exposure of systemic corruption through the parcel of executive toys.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Jill attempts to comply with bureaucratic procedure by requesting a form stamp.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She exposes the system's moral decay by revealing the 'bomb' as a bribe-laden farce, asserting moral and ideological defiance.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The full network behind the bribes and the consequences of her exposure remain unclear, leaving her vulnerable to institutional retaliation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Resistance to Alliance: Jill and Sam's Shifting Bond
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jill evolves from an elusive figure of Sam's obsession into an active collaborator and intimate ally, gradually asserting emotional and moral authority in their relationship while deepening their personal and ideological connection.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Jill is a distant, mysterious figure Sam is desperate to reach.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She enters Sam's familial space, symbolizing full emotional integration and mutual commitment.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Sam's institutional loyalties and Jill's covert mission continue to create tension, threatening the stability of their bond.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Covert Operative: Smuggling Truth in the Lorry
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jill executes a clandestine mission to transport and conceal a suspicious parcel, maintaining operational secrecy under Sam's watch, demonstrating her role as a disciplined resistance agent with a defined objective beyond personal survival.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Jill retrieves a hidden package in the lorry, initiating a covert operation.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She successfully deflects suspicion and maintains control of the parcel, advancing the resistance agenda.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Sam's growing awareness and the potential exposure of her mission threaten the integrity of the operation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Ideological Awakening: Challenging the System from Within
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jill moves from being a subject of bureaucratic scrutiny to an active critic of systemic absurdity, using irony and confrontation to expose contradictions in the regime and catalyze Sam's moral awakening.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Jill's identity is uncovered through official records, marking her as a traceable entity within the system.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She challenges the logic of authority in surreal and life-threatening contexts, positioning herself as a moral and cognitive disruptor.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her ability to effect systemic change remains uncertain, as the institutions continue to operate beyond individual resistance.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Shattered Dream: Illusion vs. Reality
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sam's recurring dream of heroic liberation and romantic salvation evolves from a private fantasy into a symbolic framework for resistance, only to collapse entirely as reality repeatedly intrudes and ultimately consumes him. His goal of rescuing Jill and transcending oppression shifts from aspirational dreaming to active pursuit, then regresses into complete psychological withdrawal.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sam dreams of rescuing an idealized Jill, achieving temporary victory over oppressive forces, but is pulled back by reality.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sam retreats entirely into fantasy, becoming catatonic and unreachable, having abandoned all real-world agency.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The system remains intact and unchallenged, while Sam's final retreat raises questions about whether his fantasy is transcendence or total defeat.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Bureaucrat to Rebel: The Descent into Resistance
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sam transitions from a passive functionary disturbed by bureaucratic error to an active fugitive engaged in direct conflict with the state. His initial attempts to correct a minor administrative flaw spiral into a full-scale rebellion as he uncovers systemic violence, is targeted by the regime, and ultimately fights for survival.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sam seeks to resolve a misplaced refund cheque to avoid blame, motivated by duty and self-preservation.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sam is a hunted fugitive, escaping a destroyed government building, now fully alienated from the system he once served.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The system continues to operate beyond Sam's reach, and his resistance, though personal, has no clear impact on the larger structure.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Elusive Jill: Love as Catalyst and Mirage
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sam's relationship with Jill evolves from a distant, dream-fueled obsession into a tangible but fragile connection, repeatedly torn apart by state violence. Jill becomes both a real person and a symbolic figure of freedom and love, whose presence alternately empowers and destabilizes Sam's grasp on reality.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sam discovers Jill's flat abandoned and her car destroyed, shifting his longing into urgent pursuit.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sam wakes beside Jill, emotionally resigned and dreamless, having surrendered his internal struggle but lost his imaginative vitality.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: It remains ambiguous whether Jill's final appearance is real or another layer of Sam's delusion, leaving their relationship's authenticity unresolved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Collapse of Sanctuary: Home, Family, and Belonging
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sam's sense of personal stability and belonging erodes as his private spaces—his flat, his mother's home—are systematically violated or erased. His status shifts from a bureaucrat with a fragile but intact life to a dispossessed fugitive with no anchor in the physical world, forcing him to seek refuge in illusion.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sam's home is destroyed by absurd bureaucratic repairs, stripping him of dignity and control.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sam finds his mother's flat empty, then discovers Jill in a hidden habitation unit—his only remaining sanctuary now contingent on fugitive love.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The fate of Sam's mother and the reason for the abandonment of her flat remain unknown, symbolizing the complete erasure of his past and identity.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fractured Self: Fantasy, Identity, and Surrender
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sam's identity fractures under the pressure of a dehumanizing system, oscillating between heroic fantasy and passive victimhood. His internal arc traces the erosion of self through dream, desire, and delusion, culminating in a psychological surrender where fantasy becomes indistinguishable from reality.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sam experiences a vivid dream of heroism and love, momentarily transcending his mundane existence.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sam is mentally lost in a self-constructed dream, physically alive but psychologically absent, having fully retreated from reality.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The boundary between Sam's final dream and truth is obliterated, leaving ambiguous whether his end is liberation or annihilation.
